frame_analyses.description: Overview of laparoscopic trainer box with pegs, pattern cut, and pink knot-tying pad visible. Multiple instrument ports shown.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS trainer setup with Geyi branding visible

frame_analyses.description: Second overhead view showing multiple task stations: peg transfer (turquoise pegs), pattern cut (pink pad with spiral), and endoloop/knot-tying station.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Video establishes all five FLS tasks available on trainer

frame_analyses.description: Close-up view of hand holding laparoscopic grasper via single port trocar. Instrument properly inserted through original port.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates correct single-port trocar technique; hand positioning appropriate for laparoscopic control

frame_analyses.description: Laptop display showing the knot-tying pad with spiral pattern and two marked entry points (A and B labeled on subsequent frames). Video feed shows endoscopic view of the task area.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear visualization of the training surface; marks visible for suture placement

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view: Needle driver (left instrument) grasping needle. Blue suture visible. Attempting to pass needle through marked entry point A on the foam/silicone pad.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle held at approximately 2/3 point from tip; perpendicular approach angle to pad surface

frame_analyses.description: Needle passed through entry point A. Suture trailing behind needle. Positioning for second entry point B (approx 1-1.5cm away horizontally marked by annotation).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle trajectory; suture follows smoothly through first bite

frame_analyses.description: Needle emerging from pad after passing through first mark. Suture configuration shows entry at A, trajectory across marked distance. Second entry point B approaching.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Distance between A and B appears consistent with standard 1-1.5cm spacing; suture not twisted

frame_analyses.description: Second needle pass: needle entering at point B. Blue suture clearly visible spanning from A to B with appropriate tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry angle perpendicular to surface; needle properly grasped and advanced

frame_analyses.description: Needle fully passed through point B. Both suture ends now visible on pad surface. Suture forms clean path between two marks without visible gaps or slack.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both entry and exit points appear well-positioned; suture lies flat on pad surface without bunching

frame_analyses.description: Close-up endoscopic view: Surgeon's knot (double throw) in progress. Two instruments visible crossing over suture with tension applied. Knot forming at marked circle location.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw (double wrap) being executed; instruments show controlled tension application; knot positioned at target site

frame_analyses.description: First throw cinched and appearing secure. Suture forms clear loop structure. Instruments repositioning for second throw (single throw, alternating hand).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw appears well-formed and tight; good knot configuration emerging

frame_analyses.description: Second throw (single wrap) being applied by opposite hand. Knot structure consolidating. Suture ends clearly visible flanking the knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching demonstrated; single throw applied with controlled tension

frame_analyses.description: Third throw (single throw, alternating back to first hand) being cinched. Knot now fully formed with multiple wraps visible. Very tight appearance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand alternation executed correctly between throws; knot appears very secure with minimal slack visible

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view showing completed knot at marked circle. Scissors or cutting instrument approaching suture ends to cut excess suture material.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot cinched tightly at target location; cutting phase initiated

frame_analyses.description: Final view: Knot securely placed and tightened at marked location on foam pad. Both suture tails appear to have been cut. No visible gap at knot site.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task appears complete; knot secure; drain slit (represented by pad surface) shows no visible gap

penalties.description: Suture deviation from marks A and B cannot be precisely measured from oblique endoscopic angles; however, visual inspection suggests placement is within acceptable range (≤2mm from marks)
No visible gap detected at the knot site or along the suture path on the foam pad. Slit closure (if applicable) appears complete.
All three throws appear secure and tight. No evidence of knot slippage or loosening between throws or at completion.

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double wrap; appropriate tension; well-formed loop structure
Opposite hand correctly employed; single wrap applied with good control; knot consolidating
Hand switched back to right; final tightening executed; knot very tight at target location

suture_placement.rationale: Endoscopic view is oblique; marks are visible but precise mm measurement difficult. Visual inspection suggests acceptable placement.

drain_assessment.notes: Foam/silicone pad (representing slit Penrose drain) shows no visible gap at knot site. Surface integrity maintained.

confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional is supported by multiple overhead setup views, laptop display with task visualization, and annotated teaching frames (A, B, C labels for technique points). The actual performance portion shows clean technique, but camera angles during knot-tying are endoscopic and oblique, making precise mm-level deviation measurement impossible. Knot quality and hand switching are well-observed. Completion time of 240s is intermediate-level performance (below expert 80-120s benchmark but well within intermediate 120-240s range). Confidence reduced due to inability to verify exact suture deviations from marks.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation at mark A: endoscopic angle is oblique
Exact mm deviation at mark B: oblique camera angle prevents perpendicular measurement
Whether this is a pure performance or hybrid instructional/performance video — frames 1-4 are clearly instructional setup/overview; frames 5-15 appear to be actual performance

demonstrated_techniques: Proper needle driver grasp at 2/3 point from needle tip
Perpendicular needle entry angle to target tissue
Clean suture passage through two marked points with appropriate spacing
Surgeon's knot (double throw) formation with controlled tension
Single throw application with hand switching between throws
Knot cinching and tightening at marked target location
Suture cutting with minimal trauma to surrounding structure
verbal_cues: Annotations showing points A and B for suture entry/exit
Arrow indicators showing horizontal measurement between A and B
Labeled 'Hold Needle Technique' on close-up frame demonstrating proper needle driver positioning
Visual callouts of 'Original port' identifying entry point
common_errors_addressed: Video demonstrates avoiding superficial bites — needle enters perpendicular to pad surface
Proper tension control shown — knots are not over-tightened or under-cinched
Hand switching protocol explicitly followed between all three throws
Knot placement kept at marked location — no drift or off-target positioning

improvement_suggestions: Reduce overall task time from 240s toward intermediate benchmark (120-180s) by streamlining needle loading and throw setup phases
Further reduce time variance in throw application to achieve consistent expert-level speed (80-120s total would be competitive)
technique_summary: Well-executed intracorporeal suture task with clean knot-tying technique. All three throws successfully completed with appropriate hand switching. Knot appears secure and properly positioned. No visible gaps or drain damage. Performance falls in intermediate tier (240s completion time) but demonstrates expert-level knot quality and hand discipline.
strengths: Proper hand switching between all three throws demonstrates advanced technique understanding
Knot formation is clean and tight with no visible slippage
Suture placement shows good spatial control and two-point accuracy
Overall performance shows methodical, controlled approach without rushed movements
Video effectively demonstrates both proper technique and teaching points through annotations
